stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern, efficient model deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of text generation using transformer-based architectures.
The model is associated with Meta's Llama family, indicating relevance in the open-source LLM space.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The study identifies the 'memory curse,' where expanding LLM context windows degrades cooperative intent in multi-agent social dilemmas.
Testing across 7 LLMs and 4 games showed that increased history access negatively impacted cooperation in 18 out of 28 tested settings.
The research isolates the mechanism, linking the breakdown in cooperation to the erosion of forward-looking intent within the LLMs' reasoning traces.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The study introduces the 'memory curse,' finding that expanding LLM context windows systematically degrades cooperation in multi-agent social dilemmas.
The negative impact was observed across 7 LLMs and 4 games over 500 rounds, showing a failure in 18 out of 28 tested settings.
The research isolates the mechanism, linking the breakdown in cooperation to the erosion of forward-looking intent during reasoning traces.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets and optimizes for Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
These models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature to improve source attribution.
The update involves linking to more sources within the AI-generated summaries.
This change aims to increase transparency and verifiability in Google's AI search results.

stories.title: Rubric-Grounded RL: Structured Judge Rewards for Generalizable Reasoning
stories.author: Dan O'Malley
bullet_points: Introduces 'rubric-grounded reinforcement learning' (RL) to improve LLM training.
The core innovation is decomposing the reward signal into multiple, verifiable criteria rather than using a single holistic score.
Utilizes a frozen LLM judge to provide a structured, multi-criterion feedback mechanism (partial-credit optimization).

stories.title: Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models
stories.author: Maria Perez-Ortiz
bullet_points: The research demonstrates that the tool selection process within language models is linearly readable and steerable.
By manipulating the difference in internal activations between two tools, researchers can reliably force a model to choose a specific tool.
This work addresses the 'invisible failure' problem in tool-calling agents, offering a new method for internal model probing across major models like Gemma, Qwen, and Llama.

stories.title: GLiGuard: Schema-Conditioned Classification for LLM Safeguard
stories.author: Ash Lewis
bullet_points: Introduces GLiGuard, a new safeguard model for Large Language Models (LLMs) focused on policy-compliant outputs.
Addresses the inefficiency of current guardrails, which use large autoregressive decoders (7B-27B parameters) for a classification task.
GLiGuard achieves this goal with a significantly smaller 0.3B-parameter model, promising lower latency and better scalability for multi-aspect evaluation.

stories.title: Obsidian plugin was abused to deploy a remote access trojan
stories.author: cmbailey
bullet_points: A vulnerability in an Obsidian plugin was exploited to deploy a Remote Access Trojan (RAT).
This highlights the security risks associated with third-party plugins in personal knowledge management tools.
The incident serves as a critical warning regarding supply chain and plugin security in popular software ecosystems.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: New connected AI companions are emerging in the children's toy market.
These toys have the potential to significantly alter childhood play and routines.
The rise of these devices has prompted calls from lawmakers for potential bans.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article addresses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries.
The focus is on the difficulty in manufacturing mobile or movable qubits.
This points to a key hurdle in advancing quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Sony anticipates that 'efficient' AI tools will significantly increase the volume of games released to the market.
Despite the rise of AI-assisted creation, Sony emphasizes that human artists must remain central to the development process.
The news signals an industry trend where AI acts as an accelerator for content creation rather than a complete replacement for human creativity.

stories.title: EmambaIR: Efficient Visual State Space Model for Event-guided Image Reconstruction
stories.author: Yunhang Qian
bullet_points: EmambaIR introduces a new Visual State Space Model (VSSM) for event-guided image reconstruction.
It addresses limitations of existing methods, specifically the global correlation weakness of CNNs and the quadratic complexity of ViTs.
The model aims to provide an efficient alternative for high-resolution image reconstruction using event-based data.

stories.title: Flow-OPD: On-Policy Distillation for Flow Matching Models
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Flow-OPD, a novel technique applying On-Policy Distillation (OPD) to Flow Matching (FM) models.
Addresses critical bottlenecks in FM text-to-image models, specifically reward sparsity and gradient interference during multi-task alignment.
Aims to mitigate the 'seesaw effect' and reward hacking issues common when optimizing heterogeneous objectives in generative models.

stories.title: CA-SQL: Complexity-Aware Inference Time Reasoning for Text-to-SQL via Exploration and Compute Budget Allocation
stories.author: Nianwen Xue
bullet_points: CA-SQL introduces a novel pipeline to improve Text-to-SQL performance on challenging tasks like the Bird-Bench.
The core problem addressed is inadequate solution space exploration in current LLM-based Text-to-SQL methods.
The solution leverages complexity-aware reasoning by allocating compute budgets to guide the search for promising candidate queries.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: Introduces the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) to overcome the slow generation bottleneck of byte-level language models.
BLT-D, a new variant, utilizes an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective alongside standard next-byte prediction.
The research aims to achieve token-level model performance using byte-level models without relying on subword vocabularies.

stories.title: SCOPE: Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration for Complex Image Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in text-to-image models: faithfully realizing complex visual intents.
Introduces the concept of 'semantic commitments' which must be tracked across grounding, generation, and verification.
Proposes 'SCOPE' (Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration) to manage the 'Conceptual Rift' during image generation.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which relies on pairwise comparisons for aligning language models.
It proposes a method to leverage richer preference structures present in datasets containing multiple rollouts per prompt, which standard DPO ignores.
The proposed approach aims to overcome issues like discarding transitivity and introducing conflicting supervision inherent in collapsing multi-rollout data into simple pairs.

stories.title: Normalizing Trajectory Models
stories.author: Josh Susskind
bullet_points: NTM addresses limitations in few-step diffusion models, where coarse transitions break the standard Gaussian denoising assumption.
The proposed Normalizing Trajectory Models (NTM) model each reverse step using an expressive conditional normalizing flow.
NTM maintains an exact likelihood training framework, unlike existing few-step methods that sacrifice it for distillation or consistency.

stories.title: Zero-Shot Imagined Speech Decoding via Imagined-to-Listened MEG Mapping
stories.author: Shihab Shamma
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method for decoding imagined speech using non-invasive MEG recordings.
Addresses the scarcity and alignment issues of imagined speech datasets by leveraging paired listening data.
Utilizes recordings from trained musicians to enhance the accuracy of the decoding approach.

stories.title: GRAPHLCP: Structure-Aware Localized Conformal Prediction on Graphs
stories.author: Sourav Medya
bullet_points: Introduces GRAPHLCP, a new method for Structure-Aware Localized Conformal Prediction on Graphs.
Addresses the challenge of applying Conformal Prediction (CP) to Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) due to graph's combinatorial nature.
Improves upon existing methods that rely on unreliable embedding-space proximity for prediction set localization.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: Addresses the slow generation bottleneck in byte-level language models (LMs) which currently match token-level performance.
Introduces the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) with novel training and generation techniques.
Features BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a new variant trained using an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which currently relies on pairwise comparisons for model alignment.
It proposes a method to leverage richer preference structures present in datasets containing multiple rollouts per prompt.
The core problem identified is that collapsing multi-rollout data into simple pairs discards transitivity and can cause training instability.

stories.title: Don't Get Your Kroneckers in a Twist: Gaussian Processes on High-Dimensional Incomplete Grids
stories.author: Ove Christiansen
bullet_points: Introduces CUTS-GPR, a novel method for numerically exact Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) in high-dimensional spaces.
The core innovation is an extremely fast kernel matrix-vector product, achieving near-linear scaling with data size (N).
The method combines an additive kernel with an incomplete grid to achieve low-order polynomial scaling with dimensionality (D).

stories.title: PropSplat: Map-Free RF Field Reconstruction via 3D Gaussian Propagation Splatting
stories.author: Joseph Camp
bullet_points: PropSplat introduces a map-free method for reconstructing Radio Frequency (RF) fields.
It utilizes 3D anisotropic Gaussian primitives to model propagation, avoiding expensive ray-tracing or dense measurements.
This approach is particularly valuable for rapid deployments where detailed geographic data is unavailable or outdated.

stories.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
stories.author: Heng Huang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of hand-crafted Test-Time Scaling (TTS) strategies for improving LLM performance.
It introduces AutoTTS, an environment-driven framework designed to automate the allocation of computation during inference.
This framework aims to move researchers away from designing specific heuristics toward defining broader operational environments.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: The research addresses the slow generation speed of byte-level language models (LMs), which currently match token-level performance but suffer from autoregressive bottlenecks.
A new model, the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT), is introduced to overcome this generation limitation.
The BLT variant, BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), utilizes a novel auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective alongside standard next-byte prediction for faster performance.

stories.title: Position: Mechanistic Interpretability Must Disclose Identification Assumptions for Causal Claims
stories.author: Fengming Liu
bullet_points: Mechanistic interpretability papers are increasingly using causal language (e.g., circuits, mediators).
The analysis found that many papers fail to explicitly state the necessary identification assumptions for their causal claims.
Validation metrics (like faithfulness or ablation effects) are often presented as causal proof without detailing the underlying assumptions.

stories.title: Prompt AI Coding Assistants to Build Production-Ready Agents: 8 Essential Patterns
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: The article focuses on advanced prompting techniques for AI coding assistants (like Kiro, Claude Code, ChatGPT).
It details 8 essential patterns for guiding these assistants to produce production-ready software agents.
This addresses a practical, intermediate-to-advanced topic in current AI development workflows.
